Programme: SMU (Sefako Makgatho Health Sciences University) uses the standard NSC 7-point achievement level scale: L7=80-100%; L6=70-79%; L5=60-69%; L4=50-59%; L3=40-49%; L2=30-39%; L1=0-29%. Life Orientation is included in APS calculations. APS is calculated from ALL subjects presented (not best 6 only - the LO score is included). Meeting minimum requirements does NOT guarantee admission - SMU receives far more applications than available spaces and selection is highly competitive. Pre-selection is done Sept-Oct using Grade 11 results; final selection is in January after NSC release. Applications open 1 April 2025; closing date for 2026 entry is 31 July 2025. Application fee: R300 non-refundable (Standard Bank, acc 071 244 395, branch 020 909, use ID as reference). Apply online at www.smu.ac.za. Maximum of 2 programme choices per application. BSc and BSc-ECP note: multiple majors/streams available within the BSc qualification; APS requirements slightly differ between BSc mainstream and BSc-ECP. Source: 2025/2026 Prospectus.
Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery (MBChB)
Additional_Subject_Requirements: Additional Subject 1: Level 5 (60%+); Additional Subject 2: Level 4 (50%+); Life Orientation: Level 5 (60%+)
Special_Notes: MathLit not accepted. APS 38 is the minimum - actual admission levels are much higher. All core sciences at L6. LO required at L5. Duration 6 years minimum, 8 years maximum. Can specialise in various medical disciplines post-graduation. See https://www.smu.ac.za/schools/medicine/medicine-undergraduate-admission-requirements/

Programme: MBChB Extended Curriculum Programme (MBChB-ECP)
Additional_Subject_Requirements: Additional Subject 1: Level 4 (50%+); Additional Subject 2: Level 4 (50%+); Life Orientation: Level 4 (50%+)
Special_Notes: RESTRICTED: Reserved exclusively for South African Black learners from Quintile 1 and 2 schools who have NOT previously been in tertiary education AND who did not qualify for the standard MBChB. Only 50 spaces available competitively. After 1-year foundation programme, students enter mainstream MBChB. MathLit not accepted.

Programme: Diploma in Emergency Medical Care (DIP EMC)
Additional_Subject_Requirements: Additional Subject 1: Level 3 (40%+); Additional Subject 2: Level 3 (40%+)
Subject_to_Selection/Access_Extended: Selection - Medical and Physical Fitness Evaluations required
Special_Notes: All 6 subjects at L3. APS 18 minimum from NSC Diploma endorsement. Applicants must pass both Medical Fitness AND Physical Fitness Evaluations compulsorily. National Certificate applicants need 60%+ in Eng/Maths/Life Sciences/Physical Sciences. Leads to HPCSA registration as Paramedic. 2-year programme.

Programme: Higher Certificate in Emergency Medical Care (HCERT EMC)
Additional_Subject_Requirements: Additional Subject 1: Level 3 (40%+); Additional Subject 2: Level 3 (40%+)
Subject_to_Selection/Access_Extended: Selection - Medical and Physical Fitness Evaluations required
Special_Notes: MathLit accepted at L4 (vs Maths L3). Life Sciences and/or Physical Sciences at L3 - either or both accepted. NSC Higher Certificate endorsement required. APS 15 from all subjects. Must pass Medical Fitness AND Physical Fitness Evaluations. 1-year entry-level programme. Leads to HPCSA registration as Emergency Care Assistant (ECA). Pathway to Diploma in EMC and Bachelor's in EMC.

Programme: Bachelor of Dental Surgery (BDS)
Additional_Subject_Requirements: Additional Subject 1: Level 5 (60%+); Additional Subject 2: Level 4 (50%+); Life Orientation: Level 5 (60%+)
Special_Notes: MathLit not accepted. Second highest APS at SMU after MBChB. Core sciences at L6. English at L5. 5-year professional degree. Post-qualification specialisation in oral surgery, orthodontics, periodontology, prosthodontics, community dentistry etc. Contact School of Dentistry: 012 521 4803.

Programme: Bachelor of Dental Therapy (BDT)
Additional_Subject_Requirements: Two additional subjects each at Level 4 (50%+); Life Orientation: Level 4 (50%+)
Special_Notes: MathLit not accepted. All subjects at L4 including LO. APS 28. 3-year degree. Dental Therapists perform basic procedures: extractions, cleaning, polishing, X-rays. Can work in private practice, public dental services and academic dentistry. Contact School of Dentistry: 012 521 4803.

Programme: Bachelor of Oral Hygiene (BOH) - with Maths
Additional_Subject_Requirements: Two additional subjects each at Level 4 (50%+); Life Orientation: Level 4 (50%+)
Special_Notes: Pure Maths route: APS 28. All subjects at L4. 3-year degree. Oral Hygienists clean and polish teeth, take X-rays, perform preventative procedures. Can work in private practice, public dental services and academic dentistry. Contact: 012 521 4803.

Programme: Bachelor of Oral Hygiene (BOH) - with MathLit
Additional_Subject_Requirements: Two additional subjects each at Level 4 (50%+); Life Orientation: Level 4 (50%+)
Special_Notes: MathLit accepted but at very high level L7 (80%+). APS is higher (31 vs 28 with Maths) because MathLit at L7 = 7 points. All science and English subjects still at L4. Same 3-year degree and career path as BOH with Maths route. Contact: 012 521 4803.

Programme: Bachelor of Pharmacy (BPharm)
Additional_Subject_Requirements: Additional Subject 1: Level 4 (50%+) - preferably Accounting; Additional Subject 2: Level 4 (50%+) - preferably Economics; Life Orientation: Level 4 (50%+)
Subject_to_Selection/Access_Extended: Selection - Student Selection Committee screens all applicants
Special_Notes: MathLit not accepted. All core sciences and English at L5. Accounting and Economics preferred as additional subjects. 4-year programme. After qualification: 1 year internship + 1 year community service before full SAPC registration. Online Higher Certificate in Vaccinology available as a separate post-qualification short learning programme. Selection is competitive; limited places; must indicate BPharm as first choice.

Programme: Online Higher Certificate in Vaccinology (HCERT Vacc)
Min_APS: Not applicable - requires prior health qualification
English_Requirement: Not applicable - requires prior health qualification
Special_Notes: NOT a school-leaver programme. Entry requirement: Registered General Nurse and Midwife with SANC OR 3-year health sciences qualification from HPCSA-registered institution. Priority to in-service HCWs working in vaccination. Online programme (Blackboard). 2 blocks, 11 modules, 120 credits. Funding available. Contact: Nontobeko Shabangu nontobeko.shabangu@smu.ac.za 012 521 4044.

Programme: Bachelor of Diagnostic Radiography (B RAD)
Additional_Subject_Requirements: Life Orientation: Level 3 (40%+); Any two other subjects: combined minimum 6 APS points
Special_Notes: APS 16 stated is the COMBINED minimum from the 4 core subjects only (Maths+PhysSci+LifeSci+English at L4 each = 16). Full APS from all 7 subjects will be higher. MathLit not accepted. Clinical training at accredited hospitals in Gauteng, Limpopo and North West. Duration 4 years minimum, 6 years maximum. Career paths: Ultrasonography, Mammography, Radiotherapy, Nuclear Medicine, CT/MRI specialisation.

Programme: Bachelor of Nursing and Midwifery (BNAM)
Additional_Subject_Requirements: Additional Subject 1: Level 4 (50%+); Additional Subject 2: Level 3 (40%+); Life Orientation: Level 3 (40%+)
Special_Notes: MathLit not accepted. Prospectus text states 'Total Combined APS of 18' for 4 core subjects (Maths+PhysSci at L4; LifeSci+English at L5 per text body) but the APS table shows Total 26 from all 7 subjects at stated levels. The L5 for Life Sciences and English per the text body may be the actual requirement - verify with SMU directly. After qualification nurses can specialise in Community Health, Primary Health Care, Psychiatric Nursing, Critical Care, Nursing Education, Health Service Management.

Programme: Bachelor of Occupational Therapy (B OCC THER)
Additional_Subject_Requirements: Additional Subject 1: Level 3 (40%+); Additional Subject 2: Level 3 (40%+); Life Orientation: Level 3 (40%+)
Special_Notes: MathLit not accepted. Core sciences and English at L4; additional subjects at L3. APS 25. 4-year degree. Must register with HPCSA upon completion. Students exposed to patients from first year of study in community, general hospitals, psychiatric institutions and special schools.

Programme: Bachelor of Science in Physiotherapy (BSc Physio)
Physical_Sciences_Requirement: Not listed as compulsory in APS table
Additional_Subject_Requirements: Any Home Language or First Additional Language: Level 4 (50%+) compulsory; Life Orientation: Level 3 (40%+); Any two additional subjects: Level 3 (40%+) each
Special_Notes: MathLit not accepted. Physical Sciences NOT listed as a compulsory APS subject for Physiotherapy (unlike most other SMU programmes). A second language (HL or FAL) is compulsory at L4. APS 25. 4-year degree. Numerous local and international employment opportunities. Assess, treat and prevent human movement disorders.

Programme: Bachelor of Audiology (B AUD)
Additional_Subject_Requirements: Additional Subject 1: Level 4 (50%+); Additional Subject 2: Level 4 (50%+); Life Orientation: Level 4 (50%+)
Subject_to_Selection/Access_Extended: Selection - limited places; clinical observation required before selection
Special_Notes: MathLit not accepted. All 7 subjects including LO at L4. APS 28. 4-year degree. Students must observe at hospitals/private practices regarding audiologist scope of practice BEFORE initial selection. Limited intake for practical reasons. Treats hearing, balance and central auditory processing disorders.

Programme: Bachelor of Speech-Language Pathology
Physical_Sciences_Requirement: Not listed as compulsory in APS table
Additional_Subject_Requirements: Any Home Language or First Additional Language: Level 4 (50%+) compulsory; Life Orientation: Level 3 (40%+); Any two additional subjects: Level 3 (40%+) each
Subject_to_Selection/Access_Extended: Selection - limited places; clinical observation required before selection
Special_Notes: MathLit not accepted. Same APS structure as Physiotherapy (APS 25). Physical Sciences NOT listed as compulsory. A second language compulsory at L4. Students must observe clinicians before initial selection. Limited intake. Treats speech, language, voice, feeding and swallowing disorders.

Programme: Bachelor of Science in Dietetics (BSc Dietetics)
Additional_Subject_Requirements: Additional Subject 1: Level 3 (40%+); Additional Subject 2: Level 3 (40%+); Life Orientation: Level 3 (40%+)
Special_Notes: MathLit not accepted. Same APS structure as OT (APS 25). Core sciences at L4; additional subjects at L3. 4-year degree including 34 weeks integrated internship (23 weeks continuous in final year). Community service (1 year) required after qualification. Must register with HPCSA. Dietitians work in hospitals, food services, pharmaceutical companies and private practice.

Programme: Bachelor of Science (BSc) - Mainstream
Min_APS: Not specified as composite total - see subject levels
Additional_Subject_Requirements: Any two other NSC subjects at Level 4 (50%+) each. NSC Bachelor pass required.
Special_Notes: MathLit not accepted. Maths required at higher L5 for mainstream BSc vs L4 for BSc-ECP. All other sciences at L4. NSC Bachelor pass required (not Diploma pass). Available majors/streams: Chemistry and Chemical Technology; Biochemistry and Chemical Technology; Physiology; Psychology; Physics; Biology and Environmental Sciences; Mathematics and Applied Mathematics; Statistics and Operations Research; Computer Science.

Programme: BSc Extended Curriculum Programme (BSc-ECP)
Min_APS: Not specified as composite total - see subject levels
Additional_Subject_Requirements: Any two other NSC subjects at Level 4 (50%+) each. NSC Bachelor pass required.
Special_Notes: MathLit not accepted. Maths at L4 vs L5 for mainstream BSc. Extended 4-year programme for students who meet L4 Maths but not L5. After completing ECP II (year 2), top ECP students may apply to transfer to BDS or BPharm if space allows. Same subject streams as mainstream BSc. NSC Bachelor pass required.
